nodes.name: Data Ingest
nodes.tagline: Live market data captured across Deriv instruments
nodes.logic: Fetches one record per instrument per pipeline run. Deriv instruments covered include Synthetic Indices (Volatility 75, Volatility 100, Boom 300/500/1000, Crash 300/500/1000, Step Index), Commodities (Gold, Silver, Oil, Natural Gas), Forex major and minor pairs, Stock Indices (S&P 500, FTSE 100, DAX 40, Nasdaq), and CFD Stocks. Tracks null fields automatically. Feeds the null triage node with a completeness score (0–100). This is the entry point — without it, nothing runs.
nodes.failure_mode: Hard abort — no data means no signal. Deriv WebSocket disconnections are handled with automatic reconnection and exponential backoff. Rate limits on fallback providers are retried with jitter.
nodes.insight: Every pipeline run starts here. Deriv Synthetic Indices such as Volatility 75 (V75) run 24/7 including weekends, making this node active at all hours — not just during traditional market sessions.

nodes.name: Null Triage
nodes.tagline: Detects missing fields and routes to cheapest filler
nodes.logic: Scans the ingested record for null fields. Categorises each missing field by which provider can fill it most cheaply. Builds a priority-ordered enrichment queue — Finnhub free first, then FMP, then Alpha Vantage, then Claude AI as last resort. Avoids paying for data you already have. Deriv Synthetic Indices legitimately lack fundamental data (P/E ratio, earnings) — triage flags these fields as structurally unavailable rather than routing them to paid enrichers.
nodes.failure_mode: Non-blocking. If triage fails, enrichers are called in default order. Minor cost inefficiency only.
nodes.insight: Synthetic Indices on Deriv have no underlying fundamentals — triage prevents wasted API calls for fields that will never exist for those instruments, keeping enrichment costs low.

nodes.name: Multi-Provider Enrichers
nodes.tagline: Parallel null-filling waterfall across paid data providers
nodes.logic: Three enrichment providers run in parallel after triage. Finnhub fills analyst ratings and insider transactions for real-market instruments (stocks, commodities). FMP fills fundamentals, earnings estimates, and ratios. Alpha Vantage fills OHLCV bars and technical indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, EMA, ATR, ADX). Results are deep-merged into a unified enriched record. For Deriv Synthetic Indices, enrichment is limited to technical indicators only — no fundamental data applies. Fields already present are never overwritten.
nodes.failure_mode: Partial tolerance. If one provider fails, others continue. The enriched record is marked with completeness_pct and which fields remain null.
nodes.insight: Enrichment quality is highest for Deriv Commodities and Forex pairs where all three providers have coverage. Deriv Synthetic Indices rely entirely on technical indicator enrichment — no fundamentals exist for algorithmic assets.

nodes.name: Deriv Price Stream
nodes.tagline: Real-time tick stream and bid/ask depth from Deriv WebSocket API
nodes.logic: Subscribes to Deriv's WebSocket tick stream for the target instrument. Fetches the last 100 ticks via ticks_history to assess short-term price momentum and micro-structure. Requests a live price proposal from Deriv to capture current bid/ask spread and contract pricing. The spread and tick velocity are fed into confidence scoring with a 20% weight on volume confirmation. For Deriv Synthetic Indices, tick data is continuous 24/7. For Commodities and Stock Indices, stream is active during market hours only.
nodes.failure_mode: Graceful degrade. If the Deriv WebSocket stream drops, the node attempts reconnect once. If unavailable, confidence node receives volume_confirmation = 0 and gate threshold tightens.
nodes.insight: Deriv Synthetic Indices generate ticks even on weekends — this node is the only data source active for those instruments outside standard market hours, giving the pipeline a 24/7 trading capability unavailable on traditional brokers.

nodes.name: Options Greeks Node
nodes.tagline: Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega — live volatility surface for real-market instruments
providers: Deriv Multiplier proposals (implied leverage as vol proxy)
nodes.logic: For real-market instruments (Commodities, Forex, Stock Indices traded via CFD), fetches options Greeks for the at-the-money contract nearest expiry via ORATS. IV rank and IV percentile flag elevated volatility setups. For Deriv Synthetic Indices where listed options don't exist, this node derives an implied volatility proxy by requesting Multiplier contract proposals at different stake levels and computing the implied cost-of-carry. This synthetic IV feeds downstream into sentiment and confidence scoring as a volatility context signal.
nodes.failure_mode: Non-blocking. If ORATS is unavailable, Greeks are nulled and confidence node runs without IV context. For Synthetic Index trades, the Multiplier-derived IV proxy continues as a fallback.
nodes.insight: Active from the $2K institutional tier onward. For Deriv Volatility 75 Index specifically, the Multiplier IV proxy has proven a reliable signal — high implied leverage cost on Multiplier contracts correlates with upcoming volatility expansion events.

nodes.name: Regime Detection AI
nodes.tagline: Classifies current market state every 15 minutes
nodes.logic: Claude analyses ADX value, VIX level, market breadth indicator, and 20-day rolling volatility to classify the market into one of 7 states: trending_bull, trending_bear, mean_reverting, high_volatility, low_volatility, crisis, or undefined. Each regime maps to a recommended strategy playbook (momentum, mean_reversion, hold, defensive). For Deriv Synthetic Indices, the regime is classified independently of macro conditions — purely from tick velocity, ATR, and ADX on the synthetic price series. Deriv Commodities and Forex regimes incorporate macro context (DXY, oil inventory data, Fed rate expectations).
nodes.failure_mode: Falls back to last known regime classification. If stale by >30 min, strategy defaults to 'hold' until re-classified.
nodes.insight: Deriv Synthetic Indices can transition between regimes faster than real markets because they're algorithmically generated. The Boom/Crash indices in particular oscillate between trending and mean-reverting regimes at high frequency — regime detection here prevents strategy mismatch more often than on any other instrument class.

nodes.name: AI Sentiment Scorer
nodes.tagline: News + analyst signals → bullish/bearish score with reasoning
nodes.logic: The most token-intensive node. Claude receives: latest news headlines (last 4h), analyst rating actions, insider transaction summary, and earnings surprise history. It outputs a sentiment score (−1 to +1), a label (bullish/bearish/neutral), a risk flag (low/medium/high/critical), and a black_swan_detected boolean. For Deriv Commodities (Gold, Oil, Natural Gas), macro sentiment from central bank decisions, energy reports, and geopolitical events is weighted heavily. For Deriv Forex pairs, interest rate differential news and CPI data dominate. For Deriv Synthetic Indices, macro sentiment is used only as a broad market regime overlay — not as a direct trade signal.
nodes.failure_mode: If Claude API is unavailable, sentiment is set to neutral (0.0) and confidence drops. The confidence gate becomes harder to pass, reducing trade frequency but protecting capital.
nodes.insight: For Deriv Gold (XAU/USD) trades, this node has caught USD strengthening events early — flagging risk as 'high' before price reacted. For Synthetic Indices like Volatility 100, macro sentiment acts as a background modifier rather than a primary signal.

nodes.name: Correlation Guard
nodes.tagline: Prevents over-exposure to correlated positions across Deriv instrument classes
providers: Internal — reads from PostgreSQL correlation_matrix table
nodes.logic: Computes rolling 20-day pairwise Pearson correlations across all Deriv instruments in the watchlist. If the candidate instrument has |correlation| > 0.70 with any currently open position, the correlation guard score is penalised. Known high-correlation clusters on Deriv: Gold + Silver (commodity cluster), EUR/USD + GBP/USD (major forex cluster), Volatility 75 + Volatility 100 (synthetic volatility cluster), Boom 500 + Boom 1000 (Boom index cluster). Holding multiple instruments from the same cluster is flagged as concentrated risk and feeds into confidence scoring with a 10% weight.
nodes.failure_mode: If correlation data is stale (>8h), the guard score defaults to 0.5 (neutral penalty). Threshold enforcement continues at reduced weight.
nodes.insight: Critical when running multi-position portfolios across Deriv's instrument classes. Traders often underestimate that Boom 300, Boom 500, and Boom 1000 indices move together structurally — this node prevents accumulating three effectively identical bets.

nodes.name: Confidence Gate
nodes.tagline: 72% composite threshold — the trade filter that protects capital
providers: Internal — aggregates all upstream node outputs
nodes.logic: Weighted composite of 6 factors: technical_alignment (25%), volume_confirmation (20%), ai_sentiment (20%), risk_reward_ratio (15%), regime_filter (10%), correlation_guard (10%). If composite_score ≥ 0.72, the trade passes and an action (buy/sell) with direction (long/short) is emitted. Stop-loss and take-profit levels are calculated using ATR-14 and the active regime playbook. For Deriv contract-based instruments (Multipliers, Accumulators), the output also includes recommended contract type. Signals below 0.72 are logged as 'skip' — they never execute.
nodes.failure_mode: If any upstream node fails, that factor defaults to its neutral/penalty value. The gate continues to operate — it never crashes. It may simply reject more trades on bad data days.
nodes.insight: This is the most important node. At the $2K institutional tier, all 6 factors are fully populated across all Deriv instrument classes — Synthetic Indices, Commodities, Forex, and Stock Indices — which is why win rate climbs from 60% to 68%.

nodes.name: RL Execution Node
nodes.tagline: Reinforcement learning selects optimal Deriv contract type and entry timing
nodes.logic: After the confidence gate passes, this node selects the optimal Deriv contract type and execution parameters. For Synthetic Indices (Volatility 75, Boom/Crash, Step Index), it chooses between Rise/Fall, Higher/Lower, Touch/No Touch, or Multiplier contracts based on regime, ATR, and tick velocity. For Commodities (Gold, Silver, Oil) and Forex pairs, it selects between CFD buy/sell or Multiplier. For Stock Indices (S&P 500, FTSE, DAX, Nasdaq), it executes standard CFD positions. All contracts are placed via the Deriv WebSocket API buy endpoint. Fill prices and slippage against proposal price are logged for ongoing RL training.
nodes.failure_mode: If Deriv WebSocket connection fails, the order is queued for retry (up to 3 attempts with 500ms delay). If Deriv API returns an error code, the circuit breaker is triggered immediately and the trade is abandoned.
nodes.insight: Deriv Multiplier contracts on Volatility 75 allow stop-loss and take-profit to be set at contract creation — this node exploits that feature to lock in exact R:R ratios before the position is live, eliminating manual management slippage entirely.

nodes.name: Circuit Breaker
nodes.tagline: Autonomous kill switch — halts all trading on anomaly detection
providers: Deriv WebSocket API (cancel contract / sell position)
nodes.logic: Monitors 9 trigger conditions in real time: daily_loss_limit (2% per instrument), portfolio_var_breach (5% VaR), ai_anomaly (black_swan_detected flag from sentiment node), fat_finger_detected (stake size >5× normal), flash_crash (price movement >3% in <60s on real-market instruments), api_data_glitch (completeness_pct <40%), correlation_breach, manual_override, drawdown_limit. When triggered, it sends a sell/cancel instruction to the Deriv WebSocket API to close or cancel all active contracts, then halts new signal processing until manually resolved by an operator. Deriv Synthetic Indices that use Multipliers with built-in stop-loss are treated as auto-closed on the platform side; the circuit breaker confirms close status via contract status polling.
nodes.failure_mode: The circuit breaker is the last line of defence. It has no failure mode — it is designed to fail safe (halt all activity) rather than fail open.
nodes.insight: Has been triggered by: stale Deriv WebSocket feeds, Boom/Crash spike events that triggered fat-finger detection, and macro black swan announcements on Gold and Oil. In all cases, it prevented outsized drawdowns. This node runs on every single pipeline cycle regardless of what else is happening.

importance_rank.name: Data Ingest
importance_rank.reason: Entry point — nothing runs without it

importance_rank.name: Confidence Gate
importance_rank.reason: The filter that directly controls win rate

importance_rank.name: AI Sentiment
importance_rank.reason: 20% of confidence score, detects black swans

importance_rank.name: Regime Detection
importance_rank.reason: Prevents strategy mismatch in wrong market regime

importance_rank.name: Enrichers
importance_rank.reason: Fills the data gaps that drive signal quality

importance_rank.name: Deriv Price Stream
importance_rank.reason: 24/7 tick data for Synthetic Indices, spread signals

importance_rank.name: Correlation Guard
importance_rank.reason: Prevents concentrated exposure across Deriv instrument classes

importance_rank.name: RL Execution
importance_rank.reason: Selects optimal Deriv contract type and reduces slippage
